originalTitle: Two-thirds of Americans think AI is advancing too quickly
summary: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT's usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of […]
content: According to the latest Pew Research poll, 49 percent of Americans report using chatbots at least occasionally, but 63 percent think the tech is advancing too quickly. Overall, use of AI chatbots has increased dramatically since 2024, when only 33 percent reported using them. Specifically, ChatGPT’s usage has doubled since 2023, with 44 percent of respondents saying they’ve used it. But opinions remain negative, with only 16 percent saying that AI will have a positive impact on society. Interestingly, it’s the younger generations who both report using AI more and who are inclined to have a more pessimistic view. 66 percent of Americans between the ages of 18 and 29 report using chatbots, but 48 percent of the same age group believe AI will have a negative impact, and just 14 percent believe it will have a positive one. Progressively older generations report using chatbots less, but also have a less negative opinion of it.  [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/roughly_two-thirds_of_americans_say_ai_is_advancing_too_quickly.png?quality=90&strip=all] While the youngest respondents were most likely to have used AI at all, it’s those in the age 30 to 49 bracket who use it most, with 34 percent saying they turn to chatbots once a day or more. This is potentially driven in part by the fact that roughly four in ten Americans reported using AI for work tasks. 30 percent of Americans think that AI makes them more productive, and 28 percent believe it helps them be more informed. Though, as we know, information served up by AI can be questionable (at best), and in Pew’s 2024 study, 66 percent of US adults said they were concerned about AI spreading inaccurate information. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/younger_adults_are_more_wary_of_ais_potential_impact_on_society_and_on_them_than_older_groups.png?quality=90&strip=all]
chineseTitle: 三分之二的美国人认为 AI 发展得太快
chineseSummary: 根据皮尤研究中心的最新民意调查，49% 的美国人表示至少偶尔使用聊天机器人，但 63% 的人认为该技术发展得太快。总体而言，自 2024 年以来，人工智能聊天机器人的使用急剧增加，当时只有 33% 的人报告使用它们。具体来说，自 2023 年以来，ChatGPT 的使用量翻了一番，占 44% […]
chineseContent: 根据皮尤研究中心的最新民意调查，49% 的美国人表示至少偶尔使用聊天机器人，但 63% 的人认为该技术发展得太快。总体而言，自 2024 年以来，人工智能聊天机器人的使用急剧增加，当时只有 33% 的人报告使用它们。具体来说，自 2023 年以来，ChatGPT 的使用量增加了一倍，44% 的受访者表示他们已经使用过它。但人们的看法仍然持负面态度，只有 16% 的人认为人工智能将对社会产生积极影响。有趣的是，年轻一代既更多地使用人工智能，又倾向于持更悲观的观点。 18 岁至 29 岁之间的美国人中有 66% 表示使用聊天机器人，但同一年龄段的人中有 48% 认为人工智能会产生负面影响，只有 14% 的人认为人工智能会产生积极影响。老一代人越来越少地使用聊天机器人，但对聊天机器人的负面看法也越来越少。  [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/roughly_two-thirds_of_americans_say_ai_is_advancing_too_quickly.png?quality=90&strip=all] 虽然最年轻的受访者最有可能使用过人工智能，但使用最多的是 30 至 49 岁年龄段的受访者，其中34% 的人表示他们每天使用一次或多次聊天机器人。这可能部分是由于大约十分之四的美国人报告使用人工智能来完成工作任务。 30% 的美国人认为人工智能提高了他们的工作效率，28% 的人认为人工智能可以帮助他们获得更多信息。不过，正如我们所知，人工智能提供的信息可能存在问题（充其量是这样），在皮尤 2024 年的研究中，66% 的美国成年人表示，他们担心人工智能传播不准确的信息。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
